and where the young ones of birds and the deer roam about freely
without any fear.
[The simile in this verse has been suggested by Valmiki's verse^
(ttttt. V. 66-5).]

Or, it may be gone through in a house rendered resplendent
by the presence of charming (deer-eyed) women whose gait is im-
peded by the weight of their heavy hips, girdles and anklets, and
whose speech is as pleasing as the warbling of cuckoos.
[This is reminiscent of Kalidasa's line,
fVi^fcr i"
(f^TT. I).]
It may be done also in holy temples, near holy rivers, in gar-
dens, in attractive regions, in a place where the earth slopes down
towards the east or the north, or where the water flows from the
left to the right.

If the site selected for the holy bath be free from ashes, char-
